title: All Seven BTS Members Depart Incheon Airport for ARIRANG World Tour US Leg
detail: On the morning of April 23, 2026, all seven BTS members — RM, Jin, Suga, J-Hope, Jimin, V, and Jungkook — were photographed departing Incheon International Airport for the United States ahead of the Tampa opener of the ARIRANG World Tour North America leg. Jin drew significant fan attention at the airport wearing a black Gucci V-neck cardigan with matching pants, generating widespread social media coverage. Star News Korea published a photo dispatch confirming the members' departure. The group is scheduled to open the North American leg at Raymond James Stadium, Tampa, Florida on April 25 (three sold-out nights, April 25, 26, and 28, approximately 195,000 total fans). The departure marks the first time all seven members travel together for a US concert run since 2019.

media.caption: All seven BTS members photographed departing Incheon International Airport for the ARIRANG World Tour US leg on April 23, 2026

title: BTS Tampa Merchandise Sales Open as Fans Line Up Ahead of April 25 Tour Opener
detail: Official ARIRANG World Tour merchandise sales opened on April 23, 2026 at a large-scale tent outside Raymond James Stadium in Tampa, Florida, with Tampa Bay Boulevard remaining closed for the third consecutive day. Thousands of fans from across North America arrived to purchase official merchandise ahead of the April 25 opening night. WTSP Tampa Bay reported on the scale of the operation, noting the City of Tampa traffic advisory expected thousands of daily visitors through the run. Separately, a controversy erupted among ARMY fans over Ticketmaster practices: fans and Weverse fan-club members reported that large blocks of tickets had been withheld and that sold-out status was displayed falsely before official sales opened, generating backlash documented by Sportskeeda. Tampa Bay Boulevard is scheduled to remain closed through April 27 (the third show night), with reopening expected by April 29 at 6:00 PM.

media.caption: BTS merchandise stand outside Raymond James Stadium in Tampa draws thousands of fans ahead of three sold-out ARIRANG World Tour shows
